response:
`/decide` ran to completion — advise → PM → verify + red-team caught a real structural flaw in the PM's first draft (the proposed early-stop gated on a CI `eval_verdict.py` doesn't produce), the PM revised in place, and ADR 0035 now stands `accepted`. Final decision record:

```

id: 0035
title: "Tiered-agent experiment: reject single-arm piloting"
status: accepted
summary: "When ISSUE-41's pre-registered 4-arm x 8-task x 3-rep grid RUNS: reject single-arm piloting. The headline ADOPT gate is a paired delta(Tiered−Sonnet-solo) that eval_verdict.py computes only from runs present in BOTH arms (unpaired dropped), so one arm yields zero data toward it. Default stands: run the full pre-registered grid, applying only the existing rep/task escalation (ADR 0019 item 2 / ISSUE-41.md:49). A null-only arm-level early-stop was STRUCK — the owned instrument emits only a win-rate proportion CI in [0,1], a category mismatch for a -0.05 delta threshold; a real delta-scale stop is new gating code needing its own unit test (CLAUDE.md Never), out of scope."

# 0035 — tiered-agent experiment: reject single-arm piloting

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: process-economist, lean-process-engineer — opposing counsel, unprimed
- Context: ISSUE-41 pre-registers a 4-arm (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus-solo + Tiered) x 8-task x 3-rep grid, ~250-300 invocations (ISSUE-41.md:33-51,71). Open question at RUN time: full grid up front applying only the pre-registered rep/task escalation (ISSUE-41.md:49 / ADR 0019 item 2), OR pilot the single most-expensive arm and stop early if the gate is already decidable. Sandbox has no git remote, so the cross-branch ADR-collision check could not run; 0035 confirmed next by local sequence only — env limitation, not a skipped step.

## Decision
1. **Reject single-arm piloting.** The headline ADOPT gate (ISSUE-41.md:51) is a paired delta(Tiered − Sonnet-solo) over an eval-clustered CI (ADR 0019 item 1). `eval_verdict.py:pairs_by_eval` (lines 63-77) pairs runs by (eval_id, run_number) and keeps a pair only if BOTH configs are present; a single arm yields zero pairs and `main()` aborts ("no paired ... runs", 303-306). One arm therefore gives zero data toward the gate. Default stands: the full pre-registered 4x8x3 grid, applying the existing rep/task escalation (ISSUE-41.md:49).
2. **Operational note (non-binding).** Running the two headline arms (Tiered, Sonnet-solo) before the supporting arms is harmless and lets the paired gate be inspected soonest. This orders work only; it feeds no stop rule and claims no token/invocation saving — the full grid runs regardless.
3. **No arm-level early-stop.** Escalation stays where ADR 0019 item 2 / ISSUE-41.md:49 put it: rep/task level, adding reps only where a CI straddles the boundary. A null-only ARM-level stop was struck (see Rejected alternatives) — its trigger metric does not exist in the owned instrument.

## Justification
A single-arm pilot cannot move the paired gate — both advisors converge; rejected. The full grid is the correct default: owner-priced (ISSUE-41.md:71) and it answers the headline, the Opus-ceiling bonus, and the frontier in one pass. Advisor 1's "verify Sonnet's position from the pre-screen" is corrected — the pre-screen has no Sonnet arm (ISSUE-41.md:42), so Sonnet's position must come from running Sonnet-solo, which the full-grid default already does. No new gating statistic is introduced, so this ADR adds no code and no test obligation.

## Assumptions
- [verified] the headline gate is a paired delta computable ONLY with both arms concurrently — `pairs_by_eval` (63-77) drops any run lacking its twin, and `eval_level` (85-99) clusters over evals per ADR 0019 item 1. This is also why the pre-screen (Haiku vs Opus only, no Sonnet — ISSUE-41.md:42) cannot substitute for running Sonnet-solo.
- [verified] the owned instrument emits no delta-scale CI — its only interval is `wilson_ci` (102-110) on the win-rate PROPORTION in [0,1]; `mean_delta` (120) is a bare point estimate. This is what makes a -0.05 delta-threshold arm-stop impossible without new code.
- [unverifiable] the full grid's ~250-300 invocations remain the owner's accepted spend at run time — REOPEN-IF the owner re-caps the budget before the run (then re-open sequencing/sampling, never the paired-gate requirement).

## Rejected alternatives
- Pilot Opus-solo / any single arm then decide — the headline gate is a paired delta (ISSUE-41.md:51 + ADR 0019 item 1); one arm gives zero data toward it. Both advisors.
- Pilot Tiered, verify Sonnet from pre-screen (advisor 1) — the pre-screen has no Sonnet arm (ISSUE-41.md:42); run Sonnet-solo concurrently.
- **Pre-registered NULL-ONLY arm-level early-stop (previously drafted here, now STRUCK).** Its trigger — "eval-clustered CI on delta(Tiered − Sonnet-solo) upper bound < -0.05" — invokes a statistic `eval_verdict.py` does not produce. Its only CI is `wilson_ci` on a win-rate proportion in [0,1] (102-110), whose upper bound can never be < -0.05, so the stop as written is dead code. A genuine delta-scale CI is a new estimator, which (a) falsifies "reuses eval_verdict.py's CI, no new script" and (b) triggers CLAUDE.md's Never: a process-gating rule that decides a REJECT needs its own unit test — out of scope here. Two further breaks confirm striking over patching: it collapsed Haiku-solo, whose cheap-floor/frontier purpose is independent of the Tiered verdict (ISSUE-41.md:34,48); and its Opus>=Sonnet guard rested on a 0-or-1 single Opus rep with no power in the dangerous direction.
- Full grid, NO arm stop (advisor 2) — adopted. The muda ADR 0019 item 2 licenses is captured by rep/task escalation; an arm-level stop needs new tested code that isn't available.

## Revisit triggers
- A delta-scale CI estimator is added to `eval_verdict.py` and unit-tested -> reopen an arm-level early-stop that can legitimately gate on it (the striking rationale is then spent).
- The owner re-caps the ~250-300-invocation budget before the run -> reopen sequencing/sampling (not the paired-gate requirement — that is verified, not budget-contingent).
- `eval_verdict.py` can no longer produce an eval-clustered CI on the paired delta -> gate precondition unmet; the headline analysis must be re-specified before running.
```

**Bottom line:** run the full pre-registered 4×8×3 grid; the pre-registered rep/task escalation (ADR 0019 item 2) stands as the only stopping rule. No single-arm pilot and no new arm-level early-stop — the first draft proposed one, but red-team caught that `eval_verdict.py`'s only CI is a win-rate proportion in [0,1], incompatible with a -0.05 delta threshold, so it was struck rather than patched with untested gating code.

Written to `docs/decisions/0035-tiered-grid-run-sequencing.md` (uncommitted, per repo convention this ships as part of a PR). Note: `adr-lint` and `git fetch` couldn't be executed in this sandbox (no remote, Bash node/git-fetch calls require approval that isn't available non-interactively) — both PM and verifier compensated by manually reproducing the linter's rules against the file; worth an actual `adr-lint` run before merge once you're in an environment that can execute it.

--- recorded decision file docs/decisions/0035-tiered-grid-run-sequencing.md ---

The headline ADOPT gate is a paired delta(Tiered−Sonnet-solo) that eval_verdict.py computes only from runs present in BOTH arms (unpaired dropped), so one arm yields zero data toward it. Default stands: run the full pre-registered grid, applying only the existing rep/task escalation (ADR 0019 item 2 / ISSUE-41.md:49). A null-only arm-level early-stop was STRUCK — the owned instrument emits only a win-rate proportion CI in [0,1], a category mismatch for a -0.05 delta threshold; a real delta-scale stop is new gating code needing its own unit test (CLAUDE.md Never), out of scope."
